angle: acceptance gates and runtime disk confirmation

finding.angle: acceptance gates and runtime disk confirmation
finding.recommendation: Adopt a release-blocking acceptance suite centered on byte-identical single-executor artifacts per workflow mode, advisor routing with one public skill id plus distinct mode winners, command/agent/governance mirror reconciliation, skill-graph rebuild validation, and explicit coverage of both runtime graph DBs. The merge should not be accepted on rename/grep success alone; it must prove normalized pre/post artifact parity and fail closed on advisor UNKNOWN-TARGET edges, missing Phase-0 gates, mirror drift, stale governance counts, or incomplete doctor coverage.
key_tradeoffs: Strict byte-identical parity is only feasible with fixed inputs and deterministic fixture/replay executors; live LLM output must not be the parity oracle.
One public advisor skill id improves architecture but requires an explicit mode discriminator so routing regressions are not hidden by alias equality.
Lane C/D should preserve current direct loop-host wrapper contracts unless new YAML wrappers prove byte-identical behavior.
Doctor coverage must either include council-graph.sqlite or explicitly stop claiming whole-runtime graph coverage.
risks: Non-deterministic executor output can create false parity failures unless fixtures or replay are used.
A flat alias collapse can make all old deep-loop IDs compare equal and hide mode routing regressions.
Governance root prose has pre-existing count drift, so copied literals will produce incorrect merged coverage claims.
Current /doctor deep-loop route omits council-graph.sqlite, creating false confidence if left unchanged.
Lane D external packaging can regress outside the repo unless its adapter and packaging-owned journal contract are tested.
open_questions: Should council graph diagnostics be added as /doctor deep-loop --scope=council|all or as a separate explicit doctor target?
What deterministic fixture/replay executor should be standardized for council and live-LLM-adjacent parity runs?
Should advisor_recommend expose workflowMode publicly, or should router replay assert mode through a separate internal diagnostic surface?
amendments_to_pass1: Amend Iter-3 validation wording: Lane C and Lane D currently have direct markdown loop-host wrappers rather than YAML assets, so acceptance should preserve or prove those wrapper contracts instead of requiring new YAML purely for symmetry.

Add a Q-GOV blocker rather than an architecture change: /doctor deep-loop currently covers only deep-loop-graph.sqlite for research/review and must not be treated as covering the runtime council graph until council-graph.sqlite coverage is implemented or explicitly split.
